title: AI Coding on Android — The Complete Guide | Acode
description: Learn how to do AI-powered coding on your Android phone. Run Claude Code, Codex, and OpenCode in Acode. The only Code Editor with native AI coding agent support. Full guide with setup instructions.
heroHeadline: AI Coding on Android Starts Here
heroSubheadline: Acode is the only Android development environment that runs AI coding agents natively. Use Claude Code, Codex, and OpenCode — all on your phone. Edit AI-generated code in a real editor with syntax highlighting.
problemHeadline: AI Coding Tools Have Ignored Android
problemPoints: The AI coding revolution has transformed how developers work — but it's been a desktop-only revolution. Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Windsurf, GitHub Copilot — all designed for laptops and workstations.
If you wanted to use AI for coding on the go, your options were zero. No Android app could run AI coding agents. The tools simply didn't exist on mobile.
This left a massive gap: millions of developers with powerful Android phones, wanting AI coding assistance, with no way to access it. The AI coding revolution stopped at the desktop door.
solutionHeadline: Acode Brings AI Coding to Android
solutionPoints: Acode is the only Android app that runs AI coding agents. Its built-in Alpine Linux terminal provides the environment that Claude Code, Codex, and OpenCode need to function.
Three AI agents, one app: Install Claude Code (Anthropic), Codex (OpenAI), and OpenCode (open-source) side by side. Use the best tool for each task without switching environments.

Acode isn't just an AI terminal — it's a complete editor. AI agents generate code in the terminal. You view, edit, and enhance it in a professional editor with syntax highlighting for 100+ languages. Git, npm, SSH — everything is integrated.
And because Acode is open source and free, there's no subscription on top of your AI API costs. Pay only for the AI services you use. Your development environment is yours.

features.title: Claude Code
features.description: Anthropic's agentic AI coding tool. Edit multiple files, run commands, search codebases, and debug complex issues — all from Acode's terminal.

features.title: OpenAI Codex
features.description: OpenAI's coding CLI. Generate functions, explain code, refactor files, and get AI assistance directly in your Android development workflow.

features.title: OpenCode
features.description: The open-source AI coding agent. Free, no API markup, supports multiple AI backends. True open-source AI development on mobile.

features.title: AI Plugins
features.description: In-editor AI assistance via plugins: AcoPilot, Blackbox AI, ChatGPT, Cosmo AI. Get code suggestions without leaving the editor.

features.title: Full Development Environment
features.description: AI agents work alongside regular development tools. Use Git, npm, SSH, and your AI agent — all in the same Linux environment.

setupSteps.title: Install Acode
setupSteps.description: Download from Google Play or F-Droid. Free.

setupSteps.title: Set Up Terminal
setupSteps.description: Tap the terminal icon. Alpine Linux installs automatically.

setupSteps.title: Install AI Agents
setupSteps.description: Choose one: Claude Code (npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code), Codex, or OpenCode. Follow each tool's setup guide.

setupSteps.title: Start AI Coding
setupSteps.description: Run your AI agent in the terminal. Open files in Acode's editor with acode ..

faqs.question: Is AI coding free on Acode?
faqs.answer: Acode is free. AI agents have their own costs: Claude Code requires an Anthropic API key (pay-as-you-go), Codex requires an OpenAI API key, and OpenCode is open-source (free tool, but needs an AI backend).

faqs.question: Can I use multiple AI agents?
faqs.answer: Yes. Install Claude Code, Codex, and OpenCode all together. Switch between them based on the task. Each runs in a separate terminal tab.

faqs.question: Does AI coding work offline?
faqs.answer: Cloud-based AI (Claude, OpenAI) needs internet. If you run a local AI model in Acode's terminal (e.g., via Ollama), AI coding can work fully offline.
